sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The LEFT key moved the handle! Looking at the image, the handle is now at a position closer to the left end. Let me check the exact position.
The handle was at column 37, and I pressed LEFT 5 times. If each LEFT press moves the handle by 2 columns left, then the handle should now be at column 37 - 5*2 = 27. That's the middle column position!
Let me verify by checking the handle position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check handle position after LEFT presses
seg = current_frame.segmentation
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'b' and n['pixels'] == 2:
        b = n['boundary']
        center = ((b[0][0]+b[-1][0])//2, (b[0][1]+b[-1][1])//2)
        print(f"Handle blue at {center}")

# Also check right panel squares
print("\nRight panel squares:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] == 9:
        b = n['boundary']
        center = ((b[0][0]+b[-1][0])//2, (b[0][1]+b[-1][1])//2)
        print(f"  color={n['color']}, center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The handle is now at (19, 27), which is the middle column position. The right-middle square (55, 34) is still green (N), and all other squares are gray (g).
Now I can paint the middle column squares. I need to paint:
- Top-middle (50, 29)
- Bottom-middle (60, 29)
The center (55, 29) should remain gray.
Let me paint the top-middle and bottom-middle squares.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Handle is at (19, 27) - middle column position
# Paint top-middle and bottom-middle squares
action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 29},  # paint top-middle
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 29},  # paint bottom-middle
])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
